heading: Withholding and remittance of State income tax by Secretary of Senate
label: 4594 Withholding and remittance of State income tax by Secretary of Senate
notes: References in TextThe Standing Rules of the Senate, referred to in subsec. (e), were revised in 1979 and 2000. Provisions relating to withdrawal of papers from the files of the Senate which were formerly contained in Rule XXX of the Standing Rules of the Senate are contained in Rule XI of the Standing Rules of the Senate.
CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 60c–3 of this title

children.heading: Agreement by Secretary with appropriate State official; covered individuals
children.label: a Agreement by Secretary with appropriate State official; covered individuals

children.text: the law of any State provides for the collection of an income tax by imposing upon employers generally the duty of withholding sums from the compensation of employees and remitting such sums to the authorities of such State; and
such duty to withhold is imposed generally with respect to the compensation of employees who are residents of such State;

children.label: A
children.text: whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: who request the Secretary to make such withholdings for remittance to that State.

children.heading: Number of remittances authorized
children.label: b Number of remittances authorized
children.text: Any agreement entered into under subsection (a) of this section shall not require the Secretary to remit such sums more often than once each calendar quarter.

children.heading: Requests by individuals of Secretary for withholding and remittance; amount of withholding; number and effective date of requests; change of designated State; revocation of request; rules and regulations
children.label: c Requests by individuals of Secretary for withholding and remittance; amount of withholding; number and effective date of requests; change of designated State; revocation of request; rules and regulations

children.text: An individual whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary may request the Secretary to withhold sums from his pay for remittance to the appropriate authorities of the State that he designates. Amounts of withholdings shall be made in accordance with those provisions of the law of that State which apply generally to withholding by employers.

children.label: A
children.text: when the Secretary first enters into an agreement with a State, a request for withholdings shall be effective on such date as the Secretary may determine; and

children.label: B
children.text: when an individual first receives an appointment, the request shall be effective on the day of appointment, if the individual makes the request at the time of appointment.

An individual may change the State designated by him for the purposes of having withholdings made and request that the withholdings be remitted in accordance with such change, and he may also revoke his request for withholdings. Any change in the State designated or revocation is effective on the first day of the first month commencing after the day on which the request for change or the revocation is received in the Disbursing Office.
The Secretary is authorized to issue rules and regulations he considers appropriate in carrying out this subsection.

children.heading: Time or times of agreements by Secretary
children.label: d Time or times of agreements by Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may enter into agreements under subsection (a) of this section at such time or times as he considers appropriate.

children.heading: Provisions as not imposing duty, burden, requirement or penalty on United States, Senate, or any officer or employee of United States; effect of filing paper, form, or document with Secretary
children.label: e Provisions as not imposing duty, burden, requirement or penalty on United States, Senate, or any officer or employee of United States; effect of filing paper, form, or document with Secretary
children.text: This section imposes no duty, burden, or requirement upon the United States, the Senate, or any officer or employee of the United States, except as specifically provided in this section. Nothing in this section shall be deemed to consent to the application of any provision of law which has the effect of subjecting the United States, the Senate, or any officer or employee of the United States to any penalty or liability by reason of the provisions of this section. Any paper, form, or document filed with the Secretary under this section is a paper of the Senate within the provisions of rule XXX of the Standing Rules of the Senate.

children.heading: “State” defined
children.label: f “State” defined
children.text: For the purposes of this section, “State” means any of the States of the United States and the District of Columbia.
